title: ALP Template Catalog v1
description: Validates metadata/templates/catalog-v1.json -- the declarative catalog of project templates (app archetypes) a frontend (alp-sdk-vscode, Alp Studio, `alp new`) reads to discover 'what starter projects exist + what each needs' instead of ad-hoc example copying. Each record maps to a CANONICAL existing example under examples/ (never a copy -- the example IS the template's rendered form) and declares its support matrix, required portable-API/library/chip surface, user-owned vs SDK-generated files, tunable parameters, and test wiring. scripts/check_template_catalog.py validates the catalog against this schema AND drift-checks that every referenced example/testcase.yaml/library/chip is real, so a template can never point at a path or dependency that doesn't exist. This is the catalog + schema + drift-validation FOUNDATION (epic #610 SS3); the deterministic render/preview API (materialising a template into a fresh directory and running its test) is a separate, deliberately-scoped follow-up -- NOT part of this contract. Stability: schemaVersion 1 is additive-only (new optional fields only); a breaking change to the shape ships as schemaVersion 2.

templates.description: One entry per archetype, sorted by id for readability (not machine-enforced).

id.description: Stable catalog key a frontend selects a template by (e.g. `alp new --template <id>`, once the render follow-up exists).

archetype.description: The app-shape class this template represents. One template per archetype in v1.

example.description: Repo-relative path to the canonical example under examples/ this template projects. The template does NOT copy/fork this example -- it IS this example; the drift gate asserts the path exists and contains a board.yaml.

description.description: Customer-facing summary of what the template teaches/does, grounded in the source example's own README/board.yaml.

families.description: SoM silicon families the example builds on (derived from the SoM SKU(s) behind its `supported_boards`/`preset`, per metadata/e1m_modules/<sku>.yaml `family:`).

som_skus.description: E1M SoM SKU(s) the example is proven against -- the board.yaml `som.sku` default plus any additional SKU implied by `supported_boards`.

core_classes.description: Cortex core classes the template's cores run on (`m` = Cortex-M/Zephyr, `a` = Cortex-A/Yocto), matching metadata/schemas/library-v1.schema.json's `requires.core_class` vocabulary.

runtimes.description: Operating systems exercised across the template's cores.

portable_apis.description: Portable <alp/*.h> headers the app source `#include`s (never a chip/vendor header directly). Drift-checked to exist under include/alp/.

libraries.description: Curated library tokens the board.yaml `libraries:`/`cores.<id>.libraries:` selects (ADR 0018 vocabulary -- scripts/alp_project_emit.py's _LIBRARY_KCONFIG union metadata/libraries/*.yaml). Drift-checked against that real vocabulary.

chips.description: Chip driver tokens the board.yaml top-level `chips:` declares. Drift-checked against metadata/chips/<name>.yaml + chips/<name>/.

e1m.description: E1M pad token from board.yaml `pins:` (e.g. E1M_I2C0).

macro.description: Board-level macro the app opens the pad by (e.g. EVK_I2C_BUS_SENSORS).

doc.description: Board.yaml's inline doc string for this route, verbatim.

routes.description: E1M pad routes the example claims, transcribed verbatim from its board.yaml `pins:` block.

emit_mode.description: The metadata/emit-registry-v1.json `mode` this artefact is rendered by.

generated_artifacts.description: Build-time artefacts scripts/alp_project.py (or the orchestrator) materialises for this template; never committed to the example directory.

test_backend.description: Which harness(es) actually exercise this template today. `yocto_host` names a Linux-side slice that exists but is not covered by the Zephyr twister gate (see the record's optional `note`).

user_owned.description: Paths (relative to the example dir) a customer forking this template edits.

generated.description: Paths the SDK generates at configure/build time; a customer never edits these directly.

parameters.description: The template's tunable knobs. Deliberately minimal -- only knobs the source example's board.yaml/prj.conf genuinely exposes today, never an invented one. May be empty (e.g. the minimal archetype has none).

testcase_yaml.description: Repo-relative path(s) to the example's twister testcase.yaml/sample.yaml (a multi-slice project may carry more than one, one per Zephyr slice). Drift-checked to exist.

native_sim_scenarios.description: Twister scenario IDs (the `tests.<id>` keys) that run/build on native_sim, transcribed from testcase_yaml.

cross_compile_matrix.description: Real hardware board targets from testcase_yaml `platform_allow`, if any. Empty when the example is only twister-verified on native_sim -- never invented.

status.description: stable: the source example carries no known content-quality gap (cross-checked against the tracked issue set in scripts/check_template_catalog.py, currently grounded in #448 and #520) and its native_sim scenario passes. preview: the example has a tracked gap, or is otherwise early; `note` is then required.

note.description: Required when status is preview: cites the tracking issue/gap. Optional free-text context otherwise (e.g. explaining a partial test_backend).

default.description: The value the source example ships today. Type matches `type` (string/integer/boolean value, or a string when type is enum).

constraints.description: Validation bounds for the render follow-up. Optional; only the bounds that genuinely apply to this knob.
